items.title: BigBodyCobain/Shadowbroker
items.description: Open-source intelligence for the global theater. Track everything from the corporate/private jets of the wealthy, and spy satellites, to seismic events in one unified interface. Hook an AI agent up to have it parse through data and find previously unseen correlations. The knowledge is available to all but rarely aggregated in the open, until now.
items.rationale: Aggregates many live OSINT telemetry feeds into a unified map, pointing to demand for packaged intelligence data layers.

items.title: Show HN: Semble – Code search for agents that uses 98% fewer tokens than grep
items.description:
Hey HN! We (Stephan and Thomas) recently open-sourced Semble. We kept running into the same problem while using Claude Code on large codebases: when the agent can't find something directly, it falls back to grep, reading full files or launching subagents. This uses a lot of tokens, and often still misses the relevant code. There are existing tools for this, but they were either too slow to index on demand, needed API keys, or had poor retrieval quality.

Semble is our solution for this. It
items.rationale: Lightweight retrieval layer helping coding agents find relevant code with far fewer context tokens than grep.

items.title: Show HN: Epiq – Distributed Git based issue tracker TUI
items.description: Issue trackers typically live outside of your workflow, with poor ergonomics. Epiq aims to solve that, bringing issue tracking into your terminal. Multi-user collaboration is achieved via git using user-scoped immutable event logs that converge in memory. Put my all into it. Let me know what you think.
items.rationale: Terminal-native issue tracking using Git event logs, keeping developer workflow local and versioned.
